It feels like it imprinted upon you; some of the green sensuality of this place sprouting into your inner dark, reminding you always of sanctified orgies enacted in secret gardens, beckoning you back there, coaxing you to nourish it. If only you could delve a little bit deeper... You blink. You’re still in the break room, that humid fastness of perfumed plant and dozing divrani, lying in a pile of cushions. By Kiha, you needed that rest! What a party that was! Although ‘party’ barely does it justice, does it? You feel slightly dirty calling it that. Celebration. That’s more like it. You yawn and stretch... then frown. Something feels different — a warm, pinching sensation in your nose and [pc.ear]. You bring your finger up to your nostril, and find there a hard, round piercing. It’s attached by a slim golden chain to your earlobe, where you find a similar piercing. Green gemstones, going off your reflection on the wet tiles. They are cool to the touch, but make you feel warm for some reason. Like you really want to be touched, and to touch back. The reflection also tells you that you’re wearing something. Didn’t you fall asleep naked? Well, now you are wearing [pc.mf |a firm, felt red collar around your neck |a silken crimson headscarf over your [pc.hair] ]. Not only that, but crimson robes, lined with gold. Very fine and loose about the wrist and [pc.feet], but daringly cut around the [pc.chest] and [pc.thigh]. This is all very nice... and it makes you think about how nice it would be to dance, and join hands, and do other very lovely things, while dressed in this... but it’s time to shake off the last of your strange, steamy sleep and get back to... whatever it was you were doing before this. The thing. The quest. Where are your clothes? Where is your [pc.weapon]? “[pc.mf |Brother |Sister ]! You’re awake!” One of the divrani hurries across to you, eyes shining. “You must be a little discombobulated, waking up in your new raiments, but you were sleeping so soundly... we couldn’t wait to get you galvanized for your new role!” What in the name of Kiha is he babbling about? “Your new role, silly.” Other curvy, green-fingered devotees are crowding around you now, looking[pc.isHeight 72| up] at you with a mixture of coyness and mischief. “We, and the actuators, all agree — this is the place you will be most of Use!” “You’re soooo good at meshing, [pc.mf |brother |sister ]!” “How you dance and sing...” “...and do the other stuff...” “You thread the Chorus together like no-one else can!” “And we know you love it.” This is said in a slightly slyer tone and look. “You don’t really want to be anywhere else in the golden machine, do you?” “Nooooooooo!” This is chorused, in disbelief. You try and clear your head, which is very difficult when they’re all crowding around you like this, reminding you with their smell and tactility just how very pleasant it is to ‘mesh’ with a smooth, warm, energetic, empathetic reptile-snack. Several of them at the same time, even better. You suck in a deep breath of air... and are reminded of the pattern, the beautiful pattern which joins together everything, machine, plant and animal, which you access when you dance with them. You almost saw the whole thing, last time. You almost understood it in its mind-wiping entirety. You recognise her as the bookish one you made melt in your arms, many hours ago. She blinks[pc.isHeight 72| up] at you through her thick glasses. “One meshing? Just one. Then we’ll let you go.” You pause and gaze back at the room. The garden of Kiha, so effortlessly timeless and blissful with its falling beams of sunshine, filled with beings who have only ever offered you openness, companionship, and the touch of the divine. You sigh, toying with your golden chain distractedly, and then turn back, to a chorus of cheers, and the outbreak of the first stanza of the Kihandala. Just one more.
